transpose.pypto_blocker: BLOCKED_BACKEND (CompileFunction pass failure for large tensors)
transpose.backend_limitation_detail: Run pass failed at CompileFunction for tensors > ~1000 elements

reduce_sum.pypto_status: PASS (B=1 all shapes, FP16 accumulation precision)
reduce_sum.ascendc_status: Host fallback (identity kernel, CPU FP32 pre-reduce)

root_causes.jit_cannot_get_source: Function defined in wrong scope (heredoc/__main__). All operators now use proper .py file definitions.
root_causes.DT_FLOAT16_not_found: Incorrect constant name. PyPTO uses DT_FP16, not DT_FLOAT16.
root_causes.reduce_sum_not_found: Incorrect API name. PyPTO uses pypto.op.sum, not pypto.op.reduce_sum.
root_causes.List_instantiation_error: Used pypto.op.List() which is a type annotation, not a constructor. Use plain Python list.
root_causes.expand_one_axis_error: expand_clone requires input.ndim == len(target_shape) and only 1 dimension may change. Fixed by per-row 1D expand.
root_causes.transpose_compile_fail: Real backend limitation: PyPTO transpose CompileFunction pass fails for large tensors.

false_limitations_corrected: JIT cannot get source code → was module/JIT definition pattern, not backend limitation
expand_clone CompileFunction failure → was API call error (List vs list)
reduce_sum not available → was API name error (reduce_sum vs sum)
DT_FLOAT16 not found → was constant name error
All three operators host fallback → was incomplete impl, not hardware limitation
remaining_ascendc_work: Expand: need device-side duplicate/broadcast kernel (not host pre-expand)
Transpose: need device-side tile-based transpose kernel (not host pre-transpose)
ReduceSum: need device-side reduction kernel (not host FP32 pre-reduce)
